evaluators.name: Create Responsive Design Discussion Evaluator
evaluators.description: Checks that the user created a Web Development discussion about responsive design with the specified title, exactly two tags (CSS and Responsive Design), a body of at least 3 sentences, and enabled subscription.

evaluators.name: JavaScript Arrays Search and Bookmark Evaluator
evaluators.description: Checks that the user searched for JavaScript arrays with the correct filters, opened the top most-upvoted qualifying discussion, upvoted its original post, and bookmarked the discussion.

evaluators.name: Trending Discussion Replies and Likes Evaluator
evaluators.description: Checks that in the top trending discussion the user replied to two different users (with an @-mention in one reply) and liked exactly three different replies from three unique other users in that thread.

evaluators.name: Follow Three High-Follower Topics Evaluator
evaluators.description: Checks that the user is following exactly three topics: one UX design-related, one React-related, and one career advice-related, each with at least 500 followers.

evaluators.name: Portfolio Feedback Private Message Evaluator
evaluators.description: Checks that the user messaged a portfolio-review reply author whose reply has at least one like, using the exact subject and a 3–5 sentence body with no external links.

evaluators.name: Update Account Settings Evaluator
evaluators.description: Checks that the user set display name to 'Alex UX Researcher', wrote a 20–30 word bio, set timezone to UTC+1 equivalent, and enabled daily summary notifications.

evaluators.name: Report Spam Reply Evaluator
evaluators.description: Checks that the user reported as spam a reply containing a hyperlink with 0 upvotes in a discussion whose title includes 'spam', with the appropriate spam note.

evaluators.name: Product Feedback Poll Discussion Evaluator
evaluators.description: Checks that there is a Product Feedback discussion titled 'Which feature should we prioritize next?' with a body of at least two sentences and a 7-day single-choice poll titled 'What should we build next?' with exactly the three specified options.
